Low-pass spatial filter

Source: R/filter.R
lowpass_filter.Rd

Applies a low-pass (moving-average) spatial filter to every layer of a raster, replacing each cell with the mean of its neighbourhood. This smooths the speckle caused by residual glint and water-surface roughness before computing the ratio transform. It is a thin, documented wrapper around terra::focal().

Usage

lowpass_filter(x, w = 3)

Arguments

x

A terra::SpatRaster.

w

Neighbourhood size passed to terra::focal(). A single odd integer gives a w x w window (default 3, i.e. a 3x3 mean).

Value

A terra::SpatRaster with the same layers as x, smoothed.

See also

correct_glint(), log_ratio()

Examples

img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",
                               package = "satbathy"))
smoothed <- lowpass_filter(img[["blue"]], w = 3)
